title: Diarrhea

red_flags.text: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped adult acute diarrhea and gastroenteritis presentation terms within DHA telehealth.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped blood, dehydration, fever, nocturnal symptoms, hydration assessment, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

red_flags.text: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped safety, clinician referral, and worsening follow-up documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

investigations.text: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed stool and blood investigation fields in qualified complicated or nonviral contexts.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Adults – 41

medication.text: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped duration, frequency, consistency, blood or mucus, fever, vomiting, exposure, hydration, medicine history, presets, and structured history.
medication.evidence_extract: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

source_limitations: Children, chronic diarrhea, and non-telehealth settings are outside the exact source.

Persistent diarrhea over 6 weeks, weight loss, family history of IBD, broad abdominal examination or tenderness, previous records, calprotectin, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some follow-up fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, infection attribution, investigation selection or interpretation, treatment, antimicrobial choice, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
